title: Oldest known plague outbreak killed hunter-gatherer children
description: DNA evidence shows that plague bacteria devastated a community in Siberia more than 5000 years ago, challenging the idea that there were no major disease outbreaks before the advent of farming and large settlements
author: Michael Le Page

title: Pigeons lock their eyes in place when they are flying
description: Bird-mounted headsets and backpacks have revealed the surprising things pigeons do with their eyes when on the wing
author: Chris Simms

title: Autism and ADHD are on the rise due to widening diagnostic criteria
description: A study of 140,000 people suggests that a broadening of the diagnostic criteria for autism and ADHD explains the sharp rise in diagnoses, but that doesn't mean too many people are being told they are autistic or have ADHD
author: Caroline Williams

title: Walking shark found in Papua New Guinea is new to science
description: Hemiscyllium dudgeonae is the tenth recorded species of walking shark, which use their pectoral fins to move across reef flats, and its limited range means it may be at high risk of extinction
author: James Woodford

title: Autism may have two distinct subtypes that vary by brain activity
description: Evidence is mounting that there are distinct subtypes of autism, and now, scientists have found that the condition can vary according to the strength of people's brain connections
author: Michael Marshall

title: Arctic Ocean reaches tipping point that could be dire for marine life
description: Disappearing sea ice is letting more sunlight in the Arctic Ocean and boosting phytoplankton growth, but this has depleted a crucial nutrient, which could severely affect animals higher up the food chain
author: Alec Luhn
